_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) - affected part justifying the procedure

claim.label: Post procedure X-ray with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)

common_queries: Missing X-ray with patient ID, date, and affected side for pre-authorization.
Missing clinical photograph of affected part for pre-authorization.
Missing Detailed Indoor case papers at claim submission.
Missing Procedure / operation notes at claim submission.
Missing Post-procedure X-ray with patient ID, date, and affected side at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Osteotomy literally means "cutting of the bone."
Osteotomy can fix problems in many different bones and joints such as Hip, Knee, Spine, Jaw, Big toe, Chin.
additional_information.post_op_care: One advantage of the procedure is that, by preserving knee anatomy, a successful osteotomy may delay the need for a joint replacement for several years. Another advantage is that there are no restrictions on physical activities after an osteotomy.
